commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR groups the Windows chat slash command palette into Session/Model/Tools/Agents buckets, restyles slash rows and highlighting, adjusts default keyboard selection, and adds shared presentation tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the PR hides the no-match popup while the `slashActive` key handling path can still intercept Tab/Escape/arrow keys. I did not run the WinUI app in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can update the composer no-match keyboard path without a product or security decision.

target.title: Chat: group the / command palette by buckets with styled rows
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem:** The Windows chat `/` slash palette (added in #890) rendered a flat, alphabetically-ordered command list capped at 8 items, so late-alphabet run options (`/verbose`, `/reasoning`, `/trace`, `/usage`) were truncated off and there were no category subheadings тАФ diverging from Mac's grouped menu. - **Why it matters:** Mac parity for discovering session/run controls via `/`. The run options were effectively hard to reach from the palette. - **What changed:** Group the `/` palette by Mac's **Session / Model / Tools / Agents** buckets with uppercase subheadings and Mac-style rows (icon ┬╖ `/name` ┬╖ `[args]` ┬╖ right-aligned description ┬╖ accent-tinted `N options` badge). As you type, the list filters/ranks live, the **matched substring is highlighted** (soft accent tint + white text) in the name and description, and the keyboard-selected row **auto-scrolls** into view. The **default Enter/Tab selection is the global best match across buckets** (not just the first bucket's first row). The flyout **background matches the composer textbox** (opaque), and it **hides entirely when nothing matches**. - **User impact:** Typing `/` shows a grouped, scrollable, Mac-faithful command menu; run options appear under **MODEL**; the list narrows/ranks as you type with the strongest match kept as the default Enter target; matches are visibly highlighted. - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No custom "Run options" (тАвтАвтАв) menu (Mac has none) and no `sessions.patch` UI plumbing. The gateway command catalog, the arg-choice picker, and the Enter/Tab/Escape/loading behavior from #890 are unchanged. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor - [ ] Docs / instructions - [x] Tests / validation - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore / infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Tray / WinUI UX - [ ] Windows node capability - [ ] Local MCP / `winnode` - [ ] Gateway / connection / pairing - [ ] Setup / onboarding - [ ] Permissions / privacy / security - [x] Tests / CI / docs ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #890 (inline `/`-triggered gateway command catalog menu) - [ ] Related to a bug or regression ## Validation After code changes, on Windows ARM64 (`OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT` set to the worktree): - `./build.ps1` тАФ тЬЕ all 5 projects built (Shared, Cli, WinNodeCli, SetupEngine, WinUI) - `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore` тАФ тЬЕ **Passed: 2683, Failed: 0, Skipped: 31** - `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore` тАФ тЬЕ **Passed: 1443, Failed: 0** New focused tests in `CommandCatalogPresentationTests.cs` cover the Mac 4-bucket mapping (`Bucket`), bucket ordering (`GroupBy` with `DisplayOrder`), labels, within-group relevance preservation, and the **global-best default selection across buckets** (`GroupForPalette` / `GroupedPalette.DefaultSelectionIndex`, incl. a cross-bucket regression test). ## Real behavior proof - **Environment tested:** Windows ARM64, non-isolated local run (`./run-app-local.ps1 -NoBuild -AllowNonMain`), connected to a gateway exposing the `commands.list` catalog. - **PR head / commit tested:** `d6a2e1d4` - **Exact steps or command run:** 1. Open Chat, type `/` тАФ observe the grouped palette. 2. Arrow-navigate (тЖС/тЖУ) through the groups past the visible fold (selected row scrolls into view). 3. Type `/reas` тАФ observe live filtering down to `/reasoning` under **MODEL**, with the matched `reas` highlighted. 4. Type a non-matching token (e.g. `/zzz`) тАФ the flyout hides. - **Observed result:** Palette renders uppercase **SESSION / MODEL / TOOLS / AGENTS** subheadings; run options (`/verbose`, `/reasoning`, `/exec`, `/think`, тАж) appear under **MODEL**; each row shows `icon ┬╖ /name ┬╖ [args] ┬╖ right-aligned description ┬╖ "N options"` badge; the matched substring is highlighted (soft tint + white text); the list filters and re-ranks as you type; the default Enter target is the global best match across buckets; the flyout background matches the textbox and hides on no match. - **Screenshot/artifact links verified?** `Yes` - **Not verified / blocked:** N/A > Rubber-duck review: dual review (GPT-5.5 + Claude Opus 4.8) тАФ no blocking issues; consensus should-fixes addressed (removed the item cap so no bucket is silently dropped, preserved within-group relevance, `NormalizeKey` prefers the text-surface `Name`, defensive accent-brush cast). > ClawSweeper/Codex review: P2 selection-order finding addressed тАФ keyboard selection now pins the global best `Search` match across grouped buckets (via `GroupForPalette` / `GroupedPalette.DefaultSelectionIndex`) with a cross-bucket regression test. Re-validated green. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` (this is presentation/ordering only over the existing `commands.list` catalog; no new command is invoked) - Data access scope changed? `No` ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
target.author: bkudiess
labels: status: ЁЯСА ready for maintainer look

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
